title: commiphora rangeana
intro: Commiphora rangeana is a medicinal plant species native to the tropical forests of East Africa, belonging to the family Burseraceae. The plant's resin, known as myrrh, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, exhibiting antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Commiphora rangeana have been found to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, improving wound healing, and fighting off infections. Traditional herbal preparations of Commiphora rangeana include the resin being extracted and used in topical creams, ointments, and incense sticks, as well as being brewed into teas and infusions for internal use.
